source_pr.title: types: fix collation for binary literal
source_pr.body:
Signed-off-by: wjhuang2016 <huangwenjun1997@gmail.com>

<!-- Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close #23581 <!-- REMOVE this line if no issue to close -->

Problem Summary:
Collation is not set for the binary literal.
When call compareBinaryLiteral(), it may get the wrong result.

### What is changed and how it works?

Set collation for the binary literal.

### Related changes

- Need to cherry-pick to the release branch

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- Unit test

Side effects


### Release note <!-- bugfixes or new feature need a release note -->

- fix collation for binary literal

symptoms: tk.MustQuery("select * from t where col1 not in (0x1B,0x20) order by col1").Check(testkit.Rows("\x1e \xec 6966939640596047133")) (expression/integration_test.go)
failure_signature: tk.MustQuery("select * from t where col1 not in (0x1B,0x20) order by col1").Check(testkit.Rows("\x1e \xec 6966939640596047133")) (expression/integration_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it relied on nondeterministic result ordering (e.g., missing ORDER BY / unsorted assertions). Result ordering is not guaranteed unless explicitly enforced. This PR stabilizes it by: tk.MustQuery("select * from t where col1 not in (0x1B,0x20) order by col1").Check(testkit.Rows("\x1e \xec 6966939640596047133")) (expression/integration_test.go).
fix_pattern: tk.MustQuery("select * from t where col1 not in (0x1B,0x20) order by col1").Check(testkit.Rows("\x1e \xec 6966939640596047133")) (expression/integration_test.go)

evidence: evidence: tk.MustQuery("select * from t where col1 not in (0x1B,0x20) order by col1").Check(testkit.Rows("\x1e \xec 6966939640596047133")) (expression/integration_test.go)
